_comment: Maps card image codes (the filename stem from the onepiece-cardgame.com image URL,
e.g. 'OP01-051_p1' from '.../OP01-051_p1.png') to exact Cardrush buying-price
entry name(s) for precise per-variant price tracking.
When a card has multiple Cardrush price entries (see 'multiplePrices' below),
add an entry here to pin that image code to the correct name(s).  The script
will then build a separate price-history entry in cardrush_price_history.json
keyed by the image code, which the frontend looks up before falling back to the
shared card code.
Format – each value is either a single exact name string or a list of exact name
strings.  Every string is matched with strict equality (==) against the Cardrush
entry 'name' field, so it must be the complete, verbatim Cardrush name.
Using a list lets you group multiple Cardrush entries under one image code
(e.g. the regular and sealed variants of the same Championship card).
"<image_code>": "<exact_cardrush_name>"
"<image_code>": ["<name_1>", "<name_2>", ...]
Example:
"OP02-099_p1": "サカズキ(パラレル/illust:Anderson)"
"OP09-004_p4": ["シャンクス(CS25-26/illust:Shishizaru)", "シャンクス(未開封/CS25-26/illust:Shishizaru)"]
NOTE: The mapping 'OP13-118': 'モンキー・Ｄ・ルフィ(llust:Anderson)' uses 'llust' (missing 'i')
intentionally – this matches a known typo in the Cardrush data itself.
--- AUTO-GENERATED SECTIONS ---
The fields below (asOf, multiplePrices, multiPricePatterns, pricesWithoutCards,
pricesWithoutCardsBreakdown, cardsWithoutPrices, cardsWithoutPricesBreakdown,
cardsWithoutPricesRarityBreakdown) are written automatically by build_price_data.py.
Do not edit them manually; they will be overwritten on the next run.
multiplePrices      – cards that have more than one Cardrush price entry on the
latest scrape date.  Use the 'entries[].name' values here
as exact strings when adding mappings above.
pricesWithoutCards  – Cardrush model numbers with no matching card in the card list.
cardsWithoutPrices  – card codes in the card list with no Cardrush price data.
